behavior_summary: U8P_IRONMAN exposes a single U8P_IRONMAN::look-type process (U8P_IRONMAN::look) that presents a short inspection text to the player: "Stone throne".
There is no branching, menu interaction, or global flag manipulation in the provided excerpt — the routine's sole observable effect is emitting a bark/description and then yielding.
This behavior is typically invoked by the game's U8P_IRONMAN::look/inspect action or the engine's U8P_IRONMAN::look-type process entry (process type 0x0208).
notable_control_flow: Straight-line: enter U8P_IRONMAN::look process -> emit single Bark ('Stone throne') -> suspend/terminate the U8P_IRONMAN::look process.
No conditional logic, loops, or external calls are present in the excerpt.
open_questions: Are there any other processes or helper functions in the full object that were not included in this excerpt (use, talk, or interaction handlers)?
Do the Unknown nodes labeled "{" and "suspend" represent only expected process framing and a standard suspend, or do they encode additional engine semantics not visible in the excerpt?

main_function.name: U8P_IRONMAN::look
main_function.purpose: When the player examines or 'looks' at this entity, display a brief description/bark: 'Stone throne'.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the game's U8P_IRONMAN::look/inspect action for this entity (player uses 'U8P_IRONMAN::look' or inspects the object).
May also be invoked by engine-level U8P_IRONMAN::look/fast-area entry for U8P_IRONMAN::look-type processes (process type 0x0208).
